versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "Therefore I urge you, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service of worship."

context_lines: Romans 12:1 should be read within the flow of its chapter and the larger argument of Romans.
explanation_lines: Note: the LSB renders *latreuō* here as "spiritual service of worship" — preserving both the service dimension and the worship connection. This is the heartbeat of *latreuō*: not singing to God, but living as those commissioned by God to serve Him in the world.
Christ's sacrifice doesn't just forgive — it commissions. Cleansed from sin, the believer is empowered to offer holy service: priestly action from a purified heart, rooted in the new covenant.

To rebuild any of these four in physical form was to misunderstand what the new covenant had accomplished. This framework helps explain why the early church's rejection of sacred buildings was not poverty or circumstance — it was theological conviction.
